_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Closed reduction of joint dislocation - Knee
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 5 years' experience
clinical_indications: Knee dislocation is a relatively rare injury but an important one to recognize because coexistent vascular injury, if missed, often leads to limb loss.
The positional classification: 5 major types of positional dislocation: medial, lateral, rotatory, posterior, and anterior.
A careful vascular examination is required.

clinical_thresholds.note: after injury carries an amputation rate of greater than 80%

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

claim.label: Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Detailed Clinical notes all vitals, history signs & symptoms, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, advise for admission, and planned line of treatment?
Was clinical photograph of affected part submitted?
Was X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes and X-ray report are not indicative of closed reduction of knee joint dislocation.
Clinical notes are incomplete (missing vitals, history, signs, symptoms, physical examination, indication for procedure, planned management, or admission advice).
Clinical photograph of affected knee joint is missing.
X-ray of affected knee joint is missing or not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right).
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) are missing for the knee joint dislocation claim.
Procedure/operative notes for knee reduction are missing.
Post-procedure X-ray of the knee joint is missing or not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right).
Post-procedure clinical photograph of the knee joint is missing.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice for knee joint dislocation is missing.
Vascular examination is not documented or inadequate.
Vascular repair was delayed more than 8 hours after injury without justification, leading to potential limb loss.

clinical_key_pointers: Knee dislocation is a relatively rare injury but an important one to recognize because coexistent vascular injury, if missed, often leads to limb loss.
The positional classification: 5 major types of positional dislocation: medial, lateral, rotatory, posterior, and anterior.
A careful vascular examination is required.
Time is of utmost concern, as vascular repair delayed more than 8 hours after injury carries an amputation rate of greater than 80%.
